home.valoriSottotitolo: We come from different backgrounds, but we are moving in the same direction. Here are the principles that guide our work.
home.storiaSottotitolo: A grassroots network born from the meeting of young people, families, and associations in Rome.
home.nessunEventoInProgramma: No events scheduled at the moment — follow us on social media to stay updated on upcoming events.
home.donaTitolo: Your contribution helps the community grow
home.donaSottotitolo: Every donation helps us give a voice to Muslims in Rome, organize events, and build dialogue. Even the smallest gesture matters.
home.mediaTitolo: A trustworthy partner, moving beyond stereotypes
home.mediaSottotitolo: We highlight faces, stories, and projects — not labels — and we are available to journalists and newsrooms to ensure accurate reporting.

chiSiamo.titolo: One network, one voice, a shared responsibility
chiSiamo.ctaTitolo: Want to get to know us or collaborate?
chiSiamo.ctaSottotitolo: We are open to dialogue with citizens, institutions, and the media.

eventi.sottotitolo: Gatherings, holidays, and initiatives open to the entire city. Add the dates to your calendar and join us.
eventi.emptyTitolo: No events scheduled at the moment
eventi.emptyBody: Follow us on Instagram to stay updated on the community's next events.

news.sottotitolo: Editorials, insights, and press releases: we highlight faces, stories, and projects — not labels.
news.nessunContenuto: No content in this section for now. Check back soon.

contatti.sottotitolo: For journalists, institutions, and citizens: we are a partner open to dialogue. Write to us, we will reply.
contatti.compilaIlModulo: Fill out the form: all fields are required.
contatti.recapitiPlaceholder: Email, phone number, and headquarters address will be published soon. In the meantime, you can reach us via the form or our social media channels.
contatti.giornalistaBody: CIR offers itself as a reliable source to contextualize news, provide spokesperson contacts, data, and materials, and participate in debates.

form.erroreMessaggio: Please write a message of at least 10 characters.
form.messaggioInviato: Message sent. We will get back to you as soon as possible.
form.providerNonConfigurato: Sending not active yet: the email provider needs to be configured (RESEND_API_KEY, CONTACT_FORM_TO).
form.erroreInvio: An error occurred while sending. Please try again later or contact us on social media.

cookieBanner.descrizione: We use technical cookies necessary for the site to work and, only with your consent, statistics and marketing cookies. Details in the <link>Privacy & Cookies notice</link>.
cookieBanner.necessariDesc: Essential for the site to work. Always active.
cookieBanner.statisticheDesc: Help us understand how the site is used, anonymously.

notFound.body: The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved. Go back to the home page or explore the site sections.

error.body: An unexpected problem occurred. You can try again or return to the home page.

istituzionale.payoff: United for a strong and conscious community
istituzionale.mission: The Islamic Community of Rome (CIR) was born from the meeting of young people, families, and leaders of centers and associations. We join forces to give a voice to Muslims in the capital, defend their rights, and build, together with the entire city, a more just, peaceful, and respectful coexistence.
istituzionale.chiSiamoIntro: Today, we represent 22 Islamic associations active throughout Rome—a number that is constantly growing. We are neither a temporary project nor a symbolic initiative: we are an organized structure, a solid network, and a unified voice.
chiSiamoPunti: A network of associations pooling experiences, energy, and expertise.
A coordination body that transforms collaboration into shared responsibility.
A representation that gives Muslims in Rome a credible and recognizable voice.
A commitment to countering Islamophobia with intelligence and firmness.
A conscious Islam, in open dialogue with institutions, schools, and citizens.
Events and social projects at the service of the community and the entire city.

valori.testo: For us, Islam is light and responsibility. Prayer, the Quran, and spirituality drive us to care for the people and places we live in. Our faith must leave tangible signs of goodness, not just words.

We come from different backgrounds, cultures, and life paths. We do not want to erase this plurality; we want to coordinate it. Within CIR, everyone brings their own contribution, but we are all rowing in the same direction.

valori.titolo: Service to the community and the city
valori.testo: We were born from the Muslim community, but our vision is broader. We want to be of service to our Muslim brothers and sisters while simultaneously being a positive presence for all of Rome.

We speak with institutions, media, schools, and associations with respect, but also with dignity. We do not seek privileges: we demand justice, recognition, and genuine spaces for dialogue, without hiding who we are.

storia.testo: A clear demand grows within the Muslim community of Rome: there is a need for greater unity, coordination, and representation. The organizations exist and the work is being done, but a shared voice is missing.
Determined young people and leaders of centers and associations begin to meet regularly: how to build a coordination body that represents shared responsibility, rather than just a name?

The community joins forces to organize a major demonstration for Palestine. Previously separate entities choose to stand shoulder to shoulder: proof that the Islamic community of Rome can work in unity.
Following numerous meetings and courageous choices, the decision is made to launch the CIR project. On January 8, CIR is officially established.

perche: Highlighting faces, stories, and projects—not labels.
Serving as a reliable partner for journalists and institutions.
